Generative AI Engineer Quiz Questions

Practise Generative AI Engineer quiz questions with answer explanations connected to the Generative AI Engineer roadmap, projects and interview preparation.

  1. Home
  2. Quizzes
  3. Generative AI Engineer

Question 1: prompt design

What is the best professional habit when working on prompt design? Consider this during local development. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Use clear instructions, examples, constraints, and test cases.

Correct answer: Use clear instructions, examples, constraints, and test cases.

Explanation: prompt design creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 2: RAG architecture

What is the best professional habit when working on RAG architecture? Consider this during code review. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Evaluate retrieval quality before judging final answers.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.

Correct answer: Evaluate retrieval quality before judging final answers.

Explanation: RAG architecture creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 3: LLM evaluation

What is the best professional habit when working on LLM evaluation? Consider this after a failed deployment. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Create representative test cases with pass/fail criteria.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.

Correct answer: Create representative test cases with pass/fail criteria.

Explanation: LLM evaluation creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 4: function calling

What is the best professional habit when working on function calling? Consider this while debugging production-like data. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Validate tool inputs and outputs against schemas.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.

Correct answer: Validate tool inputs and outputs against schemas.

Explanation: function calling creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 5: prompt injection defense

What is the best professional habit when working on prompt injection defense? Consider this when explaining the work to a hiring manager. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Treat user and retrieved content as untrusted input.

Correct answer: Treat user and retrieved content as untrusted input.

Explanation: prompt injection defense creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 6: structured output

What is the best professional habit when working on structured output? Consider this while reducing operational risk. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Use schemas, validation, and retry behavior.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.

Correct answer: Use schemas, validation, and retry behavior.

Explanation: structured output creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 7: multimodal workflows

What is the best professional habit when working on multimodal workflows? Consider this when choosing between two tools. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Validate extracted fields and expose uncertainty.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.

Correct answer: Validate extracted fields and expose uncertainty.

Explanation: multimodal workflows creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 8: latency optimization

What is the best professional habit when working on latency optimization? Consider this during a security or privacy review. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Measure time to first token and total cost.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.

Correct answer: Measure time to first token and total cost.

Explanation: latency optimization creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 9: AI product UX

What is the best professional habit when working on AI product UX? Consider this while improving performance. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Show limits, sources, and user control.

Correct answer: Show limits, sources, and user control.

Explanation: AI product UX creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 10: AI observability

What is the best professional habit when working on AI observability? Consider this before publishing the portfolio project. Difficulty focus: beginner.

Answer choices

  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.
  • Monitor quality, latency, cost, and failures.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.

Correct answer: Monitor quality, latency, cost, and failures.

Explanation: AI observability creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Answer choices

  • Monitor quality, latency, cost, and failures.
  • Use the newest tool without defining the problem.
  • Skip validation because the output looks polished.
  • Optimize for demos while ignoring limitations.

Correct answer: Monitor quality, latency, cost, and failures.

Explanation: AI observability creates value when it is validated, documented, and connected to a real decision. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 18: LLM application design

A Generative AI Engineer portfolio uses LLM application design. What would make it credible to an interviewer? Consider this before publishing the portfolio project. Difficulty focus: intermediate.

Answer choices

  • A copied tutorial with renamed variables and no setup notes.
  • A tool list with no finished workflow or evidence.
  • A definition memorized without showing operational consequences.
  • A working artifact with realistic inputs, documented tradeoffs, golden datasets, retrieval evaluation, prompt regression tests, red-team cases, latency tests, and human review of edge cases, and clear impact on groundedness, retrieval precision and recall, hallucination rate, latency, token cost, drift, and user correction rate.

Correct answer: A working artifact with realistic inputs, documented tradeoffs, golden datasets, retrieval evaluation, prompt regression tests, red-team cases, latency tests, and human review of edge cases, and clear impact on groundedness, retrieval precision and recall, hallucination rate, latency, token cost, drift, and user correction rate.

Explanation: LLM application design becomes job-ready when it is connected to implementation, verification, deployment, and role-specific impact. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.

Question 19: prompt engineering

A Generative AI Engineer portfolio uses prompt engineering. What would make it credible to an interviewer? Consider this during local development. Difficulty focus: intermediate.

Answer choices

  • A tool list with no finished workflow or evidence.
  • A definition memorized without showing operational consequences.
  • A working artifact with realistic inputs, documented tradeoffs, golden datasets, retrieval evaluation, prompt regression tests, red-team cases, latency tests, and human review of edge cases, and clear impact on groundedness, retrieval precision and recall, hallucination rate, latency, token cost, drift, and user correction rate.
  • A copied tutorial with renamed variables and no setup notes.

Correct answer: A working artifact with realistic inputs, documented tradeoffs, golden datasets, retrieval evaluation, prompt regression tests, red-team cases, latency tests, and human review of edge cases, and clear impact on groundedness, retrieval precision and recall, hallucination rate, latency, token cost, drift, and user correction rate.

Explanation: prompt engineering becomes job-ready when it is connected to implementation, verification, deployment, and role-specific impact. This is why the scenario matters for Generative AI Engineer work.
